project: Hal Cloud Daytona Sandbox Orchestration v1
description: Terminal-first cloud orchestration for running hal workloads in Daytona sandboxes with contention-safe retries, idempotent GitHub side effects, dual Postgres/Turso storage adapters, and headless auth profile management.

userStories.title: [Schema] Create runs table with lifecycle and timeout fields
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want a durable run record so orchestration survives restarts and reconciles safely
acceptanceCriteria: Add runs table columns: id, repo, base_branch, engine, auth_profile_id, scope_ref, status, attempt_count, max_attempts, deadline_at, created_at, updated_at
status enum supports exactly: queued, claimed, running, retrying, succeeded, failed, canceled
Add index on (status, created_at) for claim queue scans
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create attempts table with lease heartbeat fields
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want attempt-level state so retries, stale detection, and failure analysis are explicit
acceptanceCriteria: Add attempts table columns: id, run_id, attempt_number, worker_id, sandbox_id, status, started_at, heartbeat_at, lease_expires_at, ended_at, error_code, error_message
Enforce at most one active attempt per run via constraint/index strategy
Add indexes on (run_id), (lease_expires_at), and (status)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create events table for lifecycle/audit logs
userStories.description: As an operator, I want durable timeline events so status and logs are reconstructable from storage
acceptanceCriteria: Add events table columns: id, run_id, attempt_id, event_type, payload_json, redacted, created_at
Events are append-only with no updates or deletes in normal execution paths
Add index on (run_id, created_at) for status and log timeline queries
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create idempotency keys table for PR side effects
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want persisted idempotency keys so retries cannot duplicate external GitHub actions
acceptanceCriteria: Add idempotency_keys table columns: key, run_id, side_effect_type, result_ref, created_at
Enforce unique key semantics for each side effect key
Duplicate insert attempts return a deterministic duplicate_key domain error
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profiles table with lifecycle states
userStories.description: As an operator, I want reusable auth profiles so credentials are linked once and reused headlessly
acceptanceCriteria: Add auth_profiles table columns: id, owner_id, provider, mode, secret_ref, status, max_concurrent_runs, runtime_metadata_json, last_validated_at, expires_at, last_error_code, version, created_at, updated_at
status supports: pending_link, linked, invalid, revoked
Add constraint ensuring max_concurrent_runs >= 1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Schema] Create auth profile lock table with lease fields
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want lease-based profile locks so subscription sessions are safe under concurrency
acceptanceCriteria: Add auth_profile_locks table columns: auth_profile_id, run_id, worker_id, acquired_at, heartbeat_at, lease_expires_at, released_at
Enforce no lock overcommit beyond profile concurrency limit
Add index on (auth_profile_id, lease_expires_at) for stale lock reclamation scans
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Define Store interface and shared domain errors
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a single persistence contract so orchestration logic remains DB-agnostic
acceptanceCriteria: Define Store methods for enqueue, claim, heartbeat, transition, event recording, idempotency, auth profile read/update, and lock lease lifecycle
Define shared domain errors: not_found, conflict, duplicate_key, lease_expired, invalid_transition
Store interface is used by orchestration services with no adapter-specific calls in the service layer
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Add adapter contract tests for run/attempt invariants
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want adapter-neutral tests so both databases enforce identical correctness behavior
acceptanceCriteria: Contract tests cover enqueue, atomic claim, one-active-attempt invariant, heartbeat renewal, and transition guards
Concurrency test verifies zero duplicate claims across parallel workers
Contract tests run unchanged against both Postgres and Turso adapters
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres adapter for run/attempt/event operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres support for core orchestration operations
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres methods for run enqueue, claim, transition, and attempt/event writes
Claim query is atomic and concurrency-safe
Postgres passes contract tests for run, attempt, and event scope
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Postgres adapter for auth/idempotency operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Postgres support for auth profile and side-effect safety operations
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Postgres methods for idempotency put/get and auth profile lock acquire, renew, and release
Implement auth profile status updates and metadata writeback fields
Postgres passes contract tests for auth and idempotency scope
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso adapter for run/attempt/event operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso support for core orchestration operations
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso methods for run enqueue, claim, transition, and attempt/event writes
Claim behavior matches Postgres contract semantics under contention
Turso passes contract tests for run, attempt, and event scope
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Turso adapter for auth/idempotency operations
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want Turso support for auth profile and side-effect safety operations
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Turso methods for idempotency put/get and auth profile lock acquire, renew, and release
Implement auth profile status updates and metadata writeback fields
Turso passes contract tests for auth and idempotency scope
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Build dual-adapter benchmark harness and decision report input
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want comparable measurements so default DB choice is evidence-driven
acceptanceCriteria: Harness runs the same claim, heartbeat, retry, cancel, and auth-lock contention scenarios for both adapters
Reports include correctness counters and p50/p95 latency for claim and heartbeat operations
Output format is consistent and diffable between backends
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement submit-time validation and run enqueue
userStories.description: As an operator, I want invalid runs rejected before queueing so workers execute only compatible, policy-allowed jobs
acceptanceCriteria: submit validates required fields: repo, base_branch, engine, auth_profile_id, scope_ref
submit rejects auth profiles not in linked state and rejects provider/engine mismatches
submit enforces provider policy allow/deny rules before writing queued run
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement atomic claim with lock acquisition
userStories.description: As a worker, I want claim and lock steps to succeed or fail together so no partial ownership state exists
acceptanceCriteria: Claim transaction atomically transitions one eligible run and creates an attempt row
Claim transaction acquires auth profile lock lease in the same transaction boundary
If lock acquisition fails, run claim is rolled back and run remains claimable
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement heartbeat renewals for attempts and auth locks
userStories.description: As a worker, I want periodic lease renewals so healthy work is not reclaimed
acceptanceCriteria: Worker heartbeat updates attempts.heartbeat_at and extends attempts.lease_expires_at
Worker heartbeat updates auth lock heartbeat_at and extends lease_expires_at
Missing or expired lease causes worker self-termination with explicit lease_lost event
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement stale-attempt reconciler
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stale attempts detected automatically so abandoned work can recover
acceptanceCriteria: Reconciler scans attempts with lease_expires_at < now and active status
Reconciler marks stale attempts terminal with error_code=stale_attempt
Reconciler releases stale auth locks for those attempts
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement retry/backoff scheduler
userStories.description: As an operator, I want deterministic retry policy so transient failures are retried safely
acceptanceCriteria: Retry policy supports configurable max_attempts and exponential backoff
Eligible failed runs transition to retrying and then back to queued after delay
Runs exceeding max_attempts transition to terminal failed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Enforce run hard-timeout policy
userStories.description: As an operator, I want long-stuck runs terminated so resources are bounded
acceptanceCriteria: Runs include deadline_at at submit time from policy or default
Reconciler marks runs past deadline_at as failed with error_code=run_timeout
Timeout path performs lock release and sandbox teardown orchestration hooks
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement Daytona sandbox provisioning and teardown
userStories.description: As a worker, I want deterministic sandbox lifecycle control so attempts are reproducible and clean up safely
acceptanceCriteria: Worker provisions sandbox from configured template or image and persists sandbox_id
Worker emits sandbox lifecycle events: created, ready, teardown_started, teardown_done
Teardown always executes in terminal paths: success, failure, cancel, and timeout
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement bootstrap execution pipeline inside sandbox
userStories.description: As a worker, I want a deterministic bootstrap flow so runs can start without manual setup
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline performs tool checks, repo clone/fetch, checkout target branch, git identity setup, and hal init when .hal/ is missing
Pipeline executes configured Hal command mode: --until-complete or bounded batch mode
Pipeline emits structured events for each bootstrap step
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement failure classification mapping
userStories.description: As an operator, I want consistent error classes so retry behavior is predictable
acceptanceCriteria: Define failure classes including bootstrap_failed, auth_invalid, policy_blocked, stale_attempt, run_timeout, non_retryable
Classifier maps provider, preflight, bootstrap, and runtime failures to these classes
Retry eligibility is driven by failure class mapping instead of ad hoc string matching
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement auth materialization and preflight checks
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want headless-safe credential usage so workers never require interactive browser auth
acceptanceCriteria: Worker materializes auth artifacts from secret_ref into provider paths with directories mode 0700 and files mode 0600
Worker runs provider-specific non-interactive preflight checks before Hal execution
Worker rejects incompatible profiles based on runtime metadata including OS, arch, and CLI version
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement credential writeback and auth profile versioning
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want refreshed credentials persisted safely so long runs stay valid across retries
acceptanceCriteria: Worker detects auth artifact changes and writes updated encrypted material through Auth Broker before teardown
Writeback uses optimistic version check on auth_profiles.version to prevent lost updates
Successful writeback updates last_validated_at, expires_at, and clears last_error_code
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement secret redaction pipeline for logs/events
userStories.description: As an operator, I want logs safe by default so secrets never leak in stored or streamed output
acceptanceCriteria: Redaction masks tokens, cookies, auth codes, and secret-like substrings before persistence
Redaction runs for bootstrap logs, preflight output, runtime logs, and error payloads
Fixtures containing known secrets verify only [REDACTED] appears in stored and streamed output
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [Backend] Implement idempotent GitHub side-effect executor
userStories.description: As an operator, I want PR side effects to be exactly-once from the run perspective
acceptanceCriteria: PR create, update, and comment actions require deterministic idempotency key generation
Existing key returns stored result_ref without issuing a duplicate external API call
Retry simulation confirms one PR creation across repeated worker failures
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [CLI] Add hal-cloud submit command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a submit command that clearly confirms queueing
acceptanceCriteria: Command supports --repo, --base, --engine, --auth-profile, and --scope flags
Success output includes exact fields: run_id, status, engine, auth_profile, submitted_at
Validation failures return non-zero exit code with machine-readable error code in --json mode
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [CLI] Add hal-cloud status command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want status visibility for run health and retry progress
acceptanceCriteria: Default output includes run_id, status, attempt_count/max_attempts, current_attempt, last_heartbeat_age, deadline_at
--json output returns a stable key set matching documented schema
Unknown run ID returns non-zero exit code and not_found error code
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [CLI] Add hal-cloud logs command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want ordered event and log visibility from terminal
acceptanceCriteria: logs <run-id> returns historical events in ascending timestamp order
logs <run-id> --follow streams new events until interrupted
Output preserves redaction markers and never prints raw secret values
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [CLI] Add hal-cloud cancel command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to stop active work safely and predictably
acceptanceCriteria: cancel <run-id> marks cancel intent and returns current cancel state
Workers observe cancel intent within one heartbeat interval and stop execution
Canceled runs are excluded from future claims and retries
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [CLI] Add hal-cloud auth link and auth import commands
userStories.description: As an operator, I want outside-sandbox auth setup flows so workers remain headless
acceptanceCriteria: auth link --provider --profile supports provider-specific interactive flow from operator environment
auth import --provider --profile --source imports local authenticated artifacts when link flow is unavailable
Both commands persist encrypted secret references and set profile status to linked only after validation success
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [CLI] Add hal-cloud auth status and auth validate commands
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to inspect auth profile readiness before submitting runs
acceptanceCriteria: auth status <profile> shows provider, status, lock owner, lock expiry, and compatibility metadata summary
auth validate <profile> runs provider preflight-compatible validation and updates last_validated_at
Validation failure returns non-zero exit code with auth_invalid or auth_profile_incompatible code
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: [CLI] Add hal-cloud auth revoke command
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to revoke compromised or deprecated profiles quickly
acceptanceCriteria: auth revoke <profile> transitions profile state to revoked and records an audit event
Revoked profiles cannot be selected by submit
Existing lock on a revoked profile is marked non-renewable and reclaimed at lease expiry
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
